rationale: Round-1 finding ADDRESSED. Subtest (b) now asserts the distinctive neither-set phrase `sets neither "model" nor "family"` (config_test.go:1555), byte-for-byte identical to config.go:561's error string. The count-must-be->=1 branch (config.go) emits panel.reviewers[0] + a recovery: line but never this phrase, so the test can no longer pass on a fall-through to the count branch — it now uniquely pins the neither-set branch. A clarifying comment (1550-1554) documents the rationale. Gates green: `go test ./internal/config -run TestLoad_EmptyStringModel -v` PASS for both subtests; full package PASS; `go build ./...` exit 0. Scope clean: exactly one bead impl commit c92b3d26 on bead/mindspec-r6hk.4, exactly two files changed (config.go, config_test.go) per `git show --name-only HEAD`, git status clean. Subtest (a) (empty-model-with-family resolves to family) and all non-test code unchanged. No remaining concerns.

findings.summary: Round-1: subtest (b) asserted only the generic `recovery:` token, which the count-must-be->=1 branch also satisfies, so it did not uniquely pin the neither-set branch.
findings.resolution: config_test.go:1555 now requires the distinctive `sets neither "model" nor "family"` phrase (matches config.go:561 byte-for-byte); the `recovery:` and `panel.reviewers[0]` assertions are retained. Both subtests still PASS.
